mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase corporate borrowing costs and reduce the present value of future equity cash flows, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show positive mean in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS > 4.0%, or rolling Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending earnings revisions, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal fails to predict direction with statistical significance (p > 0.05) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between real sales of final goods and housing starts, where housing starts fall while real sales remain flat or decline, signals a structural demand mismatch that suppresses equity valuations over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint signal fails to predict 60-day returns with Rank IC > 0.04, or returns are positive in >50% of divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production above 0.5% reflects expanding manufacturing capacity and supply-side resilience, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days during post-shock recovery phases.
failure_condition: Signal predicts negative returns in >55% of triggered instances during recovery regimes, or out-of-sample directional accuracy falls below 52%.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL reading above 4.0% triggers expectations of restrictive monetary policy and margin compression, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in >60% of triggered observations, or the threshold fails to separate return distributions with p < 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects robust labor market expansion and rising aggregate income, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rolling 3-year Rank IC for PAYEMS MoM changes remains below 0.03, or 60-day forward returns show no significant positive correlation with payroll growth.

mechanism_narrative: A widening gap where real sales of final goods decline faster than real GDP growth indicates weakening consumer demand relative to overall economic output, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The divergence metric fails to predict 20-day returns with directional accuracy above 53%, or returns are positive in >55% of divergence episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production growth above 0.8% month-over-month reflects strong manufacturing momentum, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days during mid-cycle expansion phases.
failure_condition: Signal yields negative 5-day returns in >50% of triggered instances during mid-cycle regimes, or walk-forward directional accuracy falls below 51%.
